english: 1. vb. to be clean, pure Dom. — 2. sbst. cleanness, purity. — 3. adj. clean, pure. Most frq. as sbst. with negation: mi-γtsaṅ-ba (mi gtsang ba) impurity, foulness, filth Dzl. and elsewh.; excrement S.g.; mi-γtsaṅ-ba rnám-pa sna-tsôgs-kyi pûṅ-po (mi gtsang ba rnam pa sna tshogs kyi phung po) heap of all kinds of filth, mass of corruption, sometimes applied to the human body Dzl.; γtsaṅ-ma (gtsang ma) adj., clean, as to the body, clothes etc.; de ni rab-bkrús γtsaṅ-ma yin (de ni rab bkrus gtsang ma yin) that man is well washed and clean S O.; γtsaṅ-btsog-méd(-pa) (gtsang btsog med pa) one that knows no difference between clean and unclean (cf. med (med)); dirty, slovenly; rude, uncouth Glr.; γtsaṅ-mar byéd-pa (gtsang mar byed pa) 1. to clean. 2. to make one's self clean, smart, tidy; *tsáṅ-ma jhé-pa* (*tsang ma jhe pa*) C., *cò-cè* (*co ce*) W. is said to be a euphemism for circumcision. — *ṡul-tsáṅ-po* (*shul tsang po*) C. one that clears his plate, empties his cup; one that does a thing thoroughly. — γtsaṅ-kàṅ (gtsang khang) Cs., com. dri-γtsaṅ-kàṅ (dri gtsang khang) v. dri (dri). — γtsaṅ-sbrá (gtsang sbra) religious purity, शुचि; γtsaṅ-sbrá-càn (gtsang sbra can) (or daṅ ldán-pa (dang ldan pa)) morally pure, γtsaṅ-sbra-méd-pa (gtsang sbra med pa) impure Do. — γtsaṅ-ris (gtsang ris) Sch.: the pure country and its inhabitants, the pure, the saints.

Ld. *tsáṅ-po* (*tsang po*) river, stream; esp. the large stream flowing through Tibet from west to east, gen. called *Yarutsaṅpo* (*ya ru tsang po*); γtsaṅ-čú (gtsang chu), resp. γtsaṅ-čáb (gtsang chab), id.
pf. γtsis (gtsis), to invite, summon, call, appoint Sch.

1. importance Cs., γtsigs(su)-če (gtsigs su che) very important Lex.; γtsigs čé-bar byéd-pa (gtsigs che bar byed pa) to make much of Cs.; Sch. also mi-γtsigs (mi gtsigs) insignificant; unapt, and ma-γtsigs (ma gtsigs) unimportant; without difficulty, whereas in one Lex. mi-γtsigs spyód-pa (mi gtsigs spyod pa) is explained by mi-rigs-pa (mi rigs pa). — 2. Pth. 85: (but as a girl was born, the king and his ministers were quite in despair, and) btsún-mo-la yaṅ tûgs γtsigs-čúṅ-bar gyur-to (btsun mo la yang thugs gtsigs chung bar gyur to) also the queen's mind was much dejected(?). — 3. Mil.: γtsigs-su ｡bébs-pa (gtsigs su 'bebs pa) frq.; by the context: to subdue, to force, compel, also with supine, ｡baṅs bgyid-par ('bangs bgyid par) to compel to obey. — 4. Sch.: γtsigs-pai blo (gtsigs pa'i blo) quick comprehension, retentive memory.
with or without mčé-ba (mche ba), to show one's teeth, to grin Glr.; rnam-par γtsígs-pa (rnam par gtsigs pa) id. Glr.

1. crown of the head, vertex Lt., spyi-γtsug (spyi gtsug) id. Glr. frq.; γtsúg-tu ｡čiṅ-ba (gtsug tu 'ching ba) to fasten on the head; fig. sá-yig γtsúg-tu bcíṅs-pai ga (sa yig gtsug tu bcings pa'i ga), cf. ťod (thod). — 2. tuft, crest, of birds Sch. — 3. whirlpool, eddy, vortex, in the water Sch.; γtsug-｡kyil (gtsug 'khyil) Wdn., also rtsub-｡kyil (rtsub 'khyil), perh. id.(?); γtsug-rgyán (gtsug rgyan) head-ornament, γtsug-(gi) nór(-bu) (gtsug gi nor bu) jewel of the head; frq. fig.: most high, most glorious among ..., c.genit.; also γtsúg-gi nór-bur gyúr-pa (gtsug gi nor bur gyur pa) Glr., = mčóg-tu gyúr-pa (mchog tu gyur pa). — γtsug-tor (gtsug tor) = ťor-còg (thor cog), उष्णीष, conical or flame-shaped hair-tuft on the crown of a Buddha, in later times represented as an excrescence of the skull itself, v. Burn. II., 558. Schl. 209.

1. sciences, 'literae'; γtsug-lág rnám-pa bcò-brgyád (gtsug lag rnam pa bco brgyad) the eighteen sciences; kyod γtsúg-lag čé-ziṅ ｡dzaṅs-pa (khyod gtsug lag che zhing 'dzangs pa) thou, who art rich in knowledge and wisdom. — 2. scientific work or works, frq.; γtsug-lag-kàṅ (gtsug lag khang) विहार, academy, convent-temple and school, cf. also gándho-la (gandho la); γtsúg-lag-mkàn (gtsug lag mkhan) or -pa (-pa) Cs. a learned man.
to bore out, scoop out, excavate Sch. (?).
pf. γtsubs (gtsubs), to rub, γtsub-ṡiṅ (gtsub shing), a piece of dry wood that is rubbed against another (γtsub-stán (gtsub stan) or -γtán (-gtan)) in order to make fire Cs.
